stories.title: SPLC asks judge to toss DOJ indictment, alleging vindictive prosecution
stories.description: Lawyers argue the 11-count case is the "culmination of a top-down, retributive campaign" directed by President Trump against political enemies.

stories.title: Cuba calls US oil blockade 'an act of war' at UN Security Council
stories.description: Foreign Minister Bruno Rodríguez Parrilla urged the international community to mobilize against what he called an unfolding humanitarian catastrophe.

stories.title: UK and Poland to sign defense treaty targeting Russian threats
stories.description: The agreement, set for Wednesday in London, will strengthen border security, combat organized crime, and jointly develop advanced weapons including air defense missiles.

stories.title: Paxton defeats Cornyn in Texas Senate primary runoff
stories.description: Trump-backed Attorney General Ken Paxton ousted four-term Sen. John Cornyn in the costliest Senate primary in U.S. history.

stories.title: UN Security Council condemns drone strike on UAE nuclear plant
stories.description: The council unanimously called the May 17 attack on Barakah a violation of international law but did not assign blame.

stories.title: US plans quarantine facility in Kenya for Ebola-exposed Americans
stories.description: The site, pending Kenyan approval, would house Americans at risk of or infected with the Bundibugyo strain spreading in the DRC, according to The Wall Street Journal.

stories.title: Two Democratic incumbents face off in Texas runoff forced by GOP redistricting
stories.description: Reps. Al Green and Christian Menefee are competing Tuesday for a single Houston seat after Republicans redrew the congressional map.

stories.title: Rubio aide Needham promoted to deputy national security adviser
stories.description: The move deepens Rubio's hold on national security policy as he continues serving as both secretary of state and acting NSA.

stories.title: DOJ sues UCLA over antisemitism in third suit against UC this year
stories.description: The lawsuit alleges the university was "deliberately indifferent" to harassment of Jewish and Israeli students during a 2024 pro-Palestinian encampment.

stories.title: Iran calls U.S. strikes a ceasefire breach as Qatar talks continue
stories.description: Tehran warned it "will not leave any act of aggression without a response" after the U.S. hit missile sites and mine-laying vessels near Bandar Abbas.

stories.title: South Carolina Senate kills Trump-backed redistricting plan
stories.description: Record early voting turnout helped sway Republicans to reject new congressional maps targeting Rep. Jim Clyburn's district.

stories.title: Florida judge allows GOP-drawn congressional map for 2026 midterms
stories.description: Voting rights groups vow to appeal to the Florida Supreme Court after a DeSantis-appointed judge declined to block the Republican-favored districts.

stories.title: Israel intensifies Lebanon strikes as US-Iran deal nears
stories.description: Netanyahu announced expanded attacks on Hezbollah as Israeli airstrikes killed at least 11 in Lebanon's Bekaa Valley amid US-Iran negotiations.

stories.title: Trump claims 'perfect' results after physical at Walter Reed
stories.description: The 79-year-old president underwent his third medical evaluation in 13 months, but no official report had been released as of Tuesday afternoon.

stories.title: Iran's judiciary blocks presidential order to restore internet
stories.description: President Pezeshkian ordered international internet access restored after a near-90-day blackout, but the judiciary suspended the body tasked with carrying it out.

stories.title: Supreme Court rejects Florida's bid to sue over immigrant truck licenses
stories.description: The justices declined to hear the state-versus-state dispute over commercial driver's licenses issued to undocumented immigrants.

stories.title: Trump administration proposes NDAs for all federal workers
stories.description: OPM published a draft nondisclosure agreement in the Federal Register on Tuesday, targeting leaks of internal government information to the media.

stories.title: Pentagon accepts SpaceX price hike for drone connectivity amid Iran war
stories.description: The military nearly doubled what it pays per attack drone after SpaceX argued its Starlink network was being used at a discount during active combat.

stories.title: BP removes chairman Albert Manifold over conduct concerns
stories.description: The board cited "unacceptable" governance lapses and appointed Ian Tyler as interim chair, just eight months into Manifold's tenure.

stories.title: Supreme Court backs Trump on immigration judge speech curbs
stories.description: The justices reversed a Fourth Circuit ruling on procedural grounds without addressing whether the speech restrictions are constitutional.

stories.title: Zelensky says US missile defense talks have stalled
stories.description: Ukraine's president declared air defense Kyiv's top priority and said he is accelerating efforts to produce anti-ballistic systems in Europe instead.

stories.title: Cuba publishes names of 2,000 pardoned prisoners
stories.description: The full list, released Monday in the official gazette, enables rights groups to verify whether any political detainees were included in the April amnesty.

stories.title: Massie files for 2028 race days after Trump-backed primary defeat
stories.description: The Kentucky congressman declined to rule out a presidential bid, saying he hasn't decided which office to seek.

stories.title: Putin signs decree canceling debts for new Ukraine war recruits
stories.description: The Kremlin is offering up to 10 million roubles in debt relief to those who enlist for at least one year, along with their spouses.

stories.title: Iran says Khamenei suffered only 'superficial' wounds in February airstrikes
stories.description: Health Ministry officials described the supreme leader's injuries as minor, contradicting months of reports that he was in a coma or lost a leg.

stories.title: Iran would reopen Strait of Hormuz 30 days after deal, Nikkei reports
stories.description: Tehran would clear mines from the strategic waterway during the post-agreement window, ending transit fees for all ships, according to the report.

stories.title: Lavrov urges Rubio to evacuate US embassy from Kyiv
stories.description: Russia warned of "systematic strikes" on Kyiv's decision-making centers and military sites, urging all foreign diplomats to leave immediately.

stories.title: Carney calls Alberta separation vote a 'dangerous bluff,' cites Brexit experience
stories.description: The prime minister drew on his time leading the Bank of England during Brexit to warn Albertans against treating the October referendum as leverage.

stories.title: AI safety filters on Meta, Google models stripped in minutes, FT finds
stories.description: Freely available software tools can remove guardrails from major AI models, enabling responses on bioweapons, malware, and exploitation.

stories.title: DOJ cites White House shooting to push for ballroom project
stories.description: The Justice Department filed a brief Sunday invoking Saturday's fatal shooting near the White House to argue for lifting the injunction on Trump's $400 million ballroom.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV calls to 'disarm' AI in first encyclical
stories.description: "Magnifica Humanitas" warns against an unchecked technology race and declares just war theory "outdated" in the age of autonomous weapons.

stories.title: Oil and LNG tankers resume Strait of Hormuz transits after three-month halt
stories.description: Limited commercial shipping is restarting through the critical chokepoint, though volumes remain far below pre-war levels.

stories.title: Inaudible sounds hidden in podcasts can hijack AI voice assistants, researchers find
stories.description: Researchers demonstrated that imperceptible audio embedded in everyday media can trick voice AI into unauthorized actions like sending emails or downloading files.

stories.title: Trump urges Arab leaders to normalize Israel ties as Iran deal nears
stories.description: Arab and Muslim leaders were silent when Trump pressed them to join the Abraham Accords during a call about the pending Iran deal.

stories.title: Russia fires Oreshnik missile in massive assault on Kyiv
stories.description: The overnight barrage of 90 missiles and 600 drones killed at least four and wounded over 80, Ukrainian officials said.

stories.title: Gunman killed after firing on Secret Service near White House
stories.description: The suspect, identified as 21-year-old Nasire Best of Maryland, opened fire at a checkpoint Saturday evening. A bystander was critically wounded.

stories.title: NATO weighs return to Iraq after wartime evacuation
stories.description: The alliance's advisory mission was fully relocated to Italy in March after Iranian strikes on bases in northern Iraq.

stories.title: Taiwan says China deployed 100-plus vessels after Trump-Xi summit
stories.description: The fleet spans from the Yellow Sea to the South China Sea and western Pacific, marking one of Beijing's largest maritime shows of force in years.

stories.title: Iranian hackers posed as recruiters to target US aviation engineers
stories.description: Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 uncovered a campaign using fake job ads and compromised video tools to infiltrate aviation and energy networks.

stories.title: Zelensky confirms strike on chemical plant 1,700 km inside Russia
stories.description: Ukraine's overnight drone campaign hit a Perm Krai chemical plant and a Novorossiysk oil terminal, with Zelensky saying production has been "completely halted."

stories.title: DOJ deletes press releases on Jan. 6 charges from its website
stories.description: NBC News found the vast majority of news releases about Jan. 6 defendants were removed, as a $1.776 billion compensation fund draws bipartisan scrutiny.

stories.title: Zelensky calls Germany's associate EU membership plan 'unfair'
stories.description: Ukraine's president told EU leaders the proposal would leave Kyiv "voiceless" and urged full membership instead.

stories.title: Democrats demand Rubio explain visa for fugitive Polish minister
stories.description: House Democrats called the expedited visa for wanted ex-minister Zbigniew Ziobro "a massive abuse of power" and demanded answers by June 21.

stories.title: U.S. has 'almost completely excluded' Israel from Iran talks, officials say
stories.description: Israeli defense officials told The New York Times that Washington sidelined Israel from negotiations, deepening the Trump-Netanyahu rift.

stories.title: SpaceX launches first Starship V3 but loses booster over Gulf of Mexico
stories.description: The 12th test flight debuted SpaceX's most powerful rocket design, but engine failures sent the Super Heavy booster crashing into the Gulf of Mexico.

stories.title: Judge tosses Abrego Garcia smuggling charges as vindictive
stories.description: The ruling ends the criminal case against the Maryland man whose wrongful deportation to El Salvador's CECOT prison reached the Supreme Court.

stories.title: Iran condemns US sanctions on its envoy to Lebanon
stories.description: Tehran called the Treasury Department's designation of ambassador Mohammad Reza Sheibani "illegal," accusing Washington of violating international law.

stories.title: Trump digs in on 'anti-weaponization' fund as GOP revolt stalls immigration bill
stories.description: Senate Republicans blocked a $72 billion immigration bill over the fund, which could pay Jan. 6 defendants. Congress left for recess with no resolution.

stories.title: US consumer sentiment hits all-time low in final May reading
stories.description: The University of Michigan index fell to 44.8, far below the preliminary 48.2 estimate, as rising gas prices and inflation fears weighed on households.

stories.title: All 'Broadview Six' charges dropped after grand jury misconduct
stories.description: A federal judge dismissed all remaining cases against the ICE protest defendants with prejudice after a closed-door hearing revealed prosecutorial errors.

stories.title: Lutnick gave $5M to House GOP weeks before Epstein testimony
stories.description: An FEC filing made public Thursday shows the commerce secretary donated to the Congressional Leadership Fund on April 1, a month before his closed-door interview.

stories.title: Russia developing seabed nuclear missiles, German investigation finds
stories.description: NATO is monitoring a secret Russian program to hide remote-controlled nuclear missiles on the Arctic Ocean floor, as Moscow wraps up its largest nuclear drills in years.

stories.title: 75% of Democratic voters oppose military aid to Israel, poll finds
stories.description: A New York Times/Siena poll shows opposition nearly doubled from 45% three years ago, reflecting deepening frustration over Gaza and the US-Israel war on Iran.

stories.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as intelligence chief, citing husband's cancer
stories.description: The director of national intelligence is stepping down effective June 30 to support her husband's battle with a rare form of bone cancer.

stories.title: Rubio says diplomatic solution with Cuba unlikely after Castro indictment
stories.description: The secretary of state cited Russian and Chinese intelligence presence in Cuba as a national security threat, leaving military action on the table.

stories.title: Sen. Sullivan asks donors for gas money after backing Iran war
stories.description: The Alaska Republican's fundraising email drew criticism as fuel prices soar due to the conflict he has repeatedly voted to continue.

stories.title: Trump adviser sought Commerce ban on Dominion voting machines
stories.description: A Reuters investigation revealed that election-security czar Kurt Olsen asked Commerce to declare Dominion components a national security risk.

stories.title: Iran seeks permanent Hormuz toll system in talks with Oman
stories.description: Tehran's new strait authority declared a "controlled maritime zone" requiring all vessels to obtain authorization before transiting.

stories.title: Lithuania confirms crashed drone was Ukrainian
stories.description: Lithuania's defense ministry said the drone that triggered a shelter alert in Vilnius was Ukrainian, as the military continued searching for it Thursday.

stories.title: Trump scraps AI executive order after tech CEO pushback
stories.description: The president called off the signing hours before a planned ceremony, saying he didn't want to jeopardize the U.S. lead over China.

stories.title: DNC releases long-delayed 2024 election autopsy report
stories.description: Chair Ken Martin reversed months of refusals, apologizing for "creating an even bigger distraction" by withholding the document.

stories.title: Iran restarted drone production during ceasefire, US intelligence finds
stories.description: New assessments say Iran could fully restore drone strike capabilities within six months, contradicting Pentagon claims of lasting damage.

stories.title: DOJ files emergency motion to restore sanctions on UN rapporteur Albanese
stories.description: After removing UN Special Rapporteur Francesca Albanese from the sanctions list under court order, the Trump administration moved Thursday to reinstate them.

stories.title: Turkey sends flights to repatriate Gaza flotilla activists detained by Israel
stories.description: Charter flights follow international outcry over Ben-Gvir's video taunting bound detainees, with multiple countries summoning Israeli ambassadors.

stories.title: Ukrainian drone strikes bring central Russia's oil refining to a standstill
stories.description: Virtually all major refineries in central Russia have halted or reduced output, disrupting a quarter of the country's refining capacity, Reuters reported.

stories.title: NATO allies bewildered by Trump's troop reversal
stories.description: Trump announced 5,000 troops to Poland weeks after ordering the same number withdrawn, stunning allies at a NATO meeting in Sweden.

stories.title: UN General Assembly votes 141-8 to back climate ruling over US objections
stories.description: The nonbinding resolution endorses the World Court's opinion that failing to protect the planet from climate change violates international law.

stories.title: EU calls treatment of Gaza flotilla activists 'completely unacceptable'
stories.description: Israel faces a diplomatic crisis after a video showed Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir taunting handcuffed activists detained from the Gaza-bound flotilla.

stories.title: Palestinian envoy drops UN vice presidency bid after US threats
stories.description: The Trump administration warned it could revoke the Palestinian delegation's US visas if Ambassador Riyad Mansour did not withdraw.

stories.title: DOJ unseals murder indictment against Raúl Castro over 1996 plane shootdown
stories.description: Federal prosecutors charged the 94-year-old former Cuban president at a Miami ceremony marking the 30th anniversary of the attack that killed four Cuban Americans.

stories.title: OpenAI prepares to submit confidential IPO filing within days
stories.description: The AI company is reportedly working with banks on a prospectus filing days after a jury unanimously dismissed Elon Musk's lawsuit.

stories.title: DHS warns it could halt customs at sanctuary city airports
stories.description: Secretary Mullin privately told travel executives that airports in eight major cities could lose international flight processing.

stories.title: Barney Frank, gay rights pioneer in Congress, dies at 86
stories.description: The former Massachusetts congressman co-authored the Dodd-Frank Act and was the first member of Congress to voluntarily come out as gay.

stories.title: Samsung union suspends strike after last-minute deal
stories.description: Nearly 48,000 workers were set to walk out Thursday before Labor Minister Kim Young-hoon brokered a tentative agreement on bonus demands.

stories.title: House GOP pulls Iran war powers vote as absences risk defeat
stories.description: Republican leaders canceled a Thursday vote on limiting Trump's Iran military campaign after party absences threatened to hand Democrats a majority.

stories.title: US and Israel planned to install Ahmadinejad after Iran war began
stories.description: A New York Times report reveals the regime-change strategy collapsed on its first day when the former Iranian president lost faith after a close-call airstrike.

stories.title: Putin hails 'unprecedented' Russia-China ties in Beijing talks with Xi
stories.description: The summit came days after Trump's own visit to Beijing, underscoring China's role as a diplomatic bridge between Washington and Moscow.

stories.title: Trump calls birthright citizenship 'a disgrace' ahead of Supreme Court ruling
stories.description: The president urged justices to "do what's right" while criticizing the court over its earlier decision striking down his tariff regime.

stories.title: Booker wins Kentucky Democratic Senate primary
stories.description: The progressive former state legislator will face Trump-backed Rep. Andy Barr in November for Mitch McConnell's open seat.

stories.title: Chile's Kast fires security minister in first cabinet shake-up
stories.description: President José Antonio Kast removed two ministers Tuesday amid sliding approval ratings and criticism over his government's handling of crime.

stories.title: Trump administration awards $2B to quantum firms, takes equity stakes
stories.description: The Commerce Department is funding nine quantum-computing companies while acquiring ownership positions, aiming to counter China's tech influence.

stories.title: Tuberville, Jones win Alabama primaries, setting up rematch
stories.description: The former Senate rivals will face off again in November after each won their party's gubernatorial primary on Tuesday.

stories.title: Drone strike kills 28 at crowded market in Sudan
stories.description: The attack on a market in West Kordofan's Ghabesh town wounded dozens more, a local rights group said, calling it a war crime.

stories.title: Iran's Supreme Leader orders enriched uranium to stay in country
stories.description: The directive contradicts a key U.S. demand and complicates peace talks that Trump said Wednesday were in their "final stages."

stories.title: Shapiro, Garrity win primaries, setting up Pa. governor race
stories.description: Democratic Gov. Josh Shapiro and Republican Treasurer Stacy Garrity cleared uncontested primaries Tuesday, officially teeing up a November showdown.

stories.title: Survivors say Army ignored medical aid request before fatal Kuwait drone strike
stories.description: Soldiers told CBS News a killed service member could have been saved had their unit received the additional medical support it requested.

stories.title: Beijing delays Pentagon official's visit over Taiwan arms deal
stories.description: China is stalling a planned trip by Elbridge Colby, the Pentagon's No. 3 official, to pressure Trump on a $14 billion Taiwan weapons package, the FT reports.

stories.title: Meta's Louisiana AI data center could cost $200B
stories.description: Bloomberg reports the Hyperion facility in rural Richland Parish will need 10 new gas plants and up to 7.5 gigawatts of power.

stories.title: Pentagon watchdog to investigate US boat strikes in Caribbean
stories.description: The Inspector General's probe is the first formal internal review of Operation Southern Spear, which has killed nearly 200 people since September 2025.

stories.title: Judge orders White House staff to comply with Presidential Records Act
stories.description: A federal judge rejected the DOJ's claim that the Watergate-era records law is unconstitutional, issuing a preliminary injunction taking effect May 26.

stories.title: China secretly trained 200 Russian troops now fighting in Ukraine, Reuters reports
stories.description: The revelation emerged as Putin arrived in Beijing for a two-day summit with Xi, marking a new threshold in the two nations' military ties.

stories.title: Pelosi endorses Connie Chan to succeed her in Congress
stories.description: The retiring former House speaker backed the San Francisco supervisor in a tight race ahead of the June 2 primary.

stories.title: Jan. 6 officers sue to block DOJ's $1.8B anti-weaponization fund
stories.description: Retired Capitol Police officer Harry Dunn and D.C. officer Daniel Hodges filed a federal lawsuit Wednesday arguing the taxpayer-backed program is unconstitutional.

stories.title: Mexico, Uruguay aid ship docks in Havana amid Cuba crisis
stories.description: The Asian Katra cargo vessel arrived Monday carrying food and hygiene supplies as blackouts exceed 20 hours daily in parts of Havana.

stories.title: Supreme Court to decide if employees can sue under Title IX
stories.description: The justices agreed Monday to hear a case testing whether college coaches and professors can bring sex discrimination claims under the federal law.

stories.title: Xi and Putin condemn Golden Dome at Beijing summit
stories.description: The two leaders signed roughly 40 agreements and issued a joint statement calling Trump's missile defense plan a threat to global stability.

stories.title: New York Times files second lawsuit over Pentagon escort rule
stories.description: The newspaper argues the policy requiring journalist escorts inside the Pentagon is "patently unconstitutional" and retaliatory.

stories.title: Jury sides with OpenAI, rejects Musk's lawsuit
stories.description: The nine-member federal jury in Oakland found Musk's case was filed too late, removing a major legal threat to OpenAI's for-profit future.

stories.title: IRGC warns war will spread beyond Middle East if US resumes strikes
stories.description: The threat came as tankers exited the Strait of Hormuz and Trump said the conflict could end "very quickly."

stories.title: ICE agent charged in shooting during Minnesota immigration crackdown
stories.description: Hennepin County Attorney Mary Moriarty announced charges Monday against the officer who shot a Venezuelan man during Operation Metro Surge.

stories.title: DOJ drops criminal charges against Adani, ending all three U.S. cases
stories.description: The dismissal, a $275 million Treasury settlement, and an SEC deal resolve all U.S. legal proceedings against the Indian billionaire.

stories.title: Pentagon warnings on Iran's air defenses shaped Trump's strike pause
stories.description: Trump called off a planned attack on Iran after Pentagon officials warned that Tehran had learned to track U.S. warplane flight patterns, the Times reported.

stories.title: Cuba warns of 'bloodbath' after U.S. drone threat report
stories.description: President Díaz-Canel said Monday that U.S. military action against Cuba would have "incalculable consequences," denying Havana poses any threat.

stories.title: Judge allows gun, notebook as evidence in Mangione trial
stories.description: The ruling is a major win for prosecutors ahead of the September state murder trial for the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.

stories.title: Trump-backed challengers oust Massie, Raffensperger in Tuesday primaries
stories.description: The president's retribution campaign swept primary night as his endorsed candidates toppled GOP critics across Kentucky and Georgia.

stories.title: Pakistan sends 8,000 troops, jets to Saudi Arabia
stories.description: Reuters reports Pakistan deployed fighter jets, drones, and a Chinese air defense system under a mutual defense pact while mediating the Iran war.

stories.title: NextEra, Dominion agree to $67B merger forming largest U.S. utility
stories.description: The all-stock deal would unite 10 million customer accounts across four states amid surging power demand from AI data centers.

stories.title: EU reaches deal to cut US import duties ahead of Trump's tariff deadline
stories.description: Negotiators from the European Parliament and Council struck a provisional agreement early Wednesday in Strasbourg, clearing the way for a ratification vote in June.

stories.title: Saudi Arabia intercepts three drones from Iraqi airspace
stories.description: The kingdom vowed to take "necessary operational measures" to defend its sovereignty as drone threats persist despite the April ceasefire.

stories.title: Two Navy Growlers collide mid-air at Idaho air show
stories.description: All four crew members ejected safely after two EA-18G Growler jets crashed during the Gunfighter Skies Air Show at Mountain Home Air Force Base.

stories.title: Trump's Gaza board warns of funding gap in UN report
stories.description: The Board of Peace said $17 billion remains pledged but did not disclose how much has been received for the $70 billion reconstruction effort.

stories.title: All 46 Council of Europe nations adopt migration declaration
stories.description: The non-binding declaration reinterprets the European Convention on Human Rights to ease deportations, including to third-country "return hubs."

stories.title: DHS forms unit targeting green card holders for deportation
stories.description: A newly created "Tactical Operations Division" has reviewed roughly 2,890 cases and is seeking to deport at least 50 lawful permanent residents.

stories.title: Trump drops plan requiring banks to collect citizenship data
stories.description: The president signed two executive orders Tuesday, replacing a months-long push for a bank citizenship mandate with softer guidance.

stories.title: US suspects Iran hacked fuel tank monitors at gas stations
stories.description: Hackers exploited unprotected automatic tank gauges across multiple states, manipulating display readings but not actual fuel levels, CNN reports.

stories.title: Kataib Hezbollah commander charged in plot against U.S. synagogue, Jewish centers
stories.description: Al-Saadi, arrested in Turkey, faces six terrorism counts for allegedly directing 20 attacks across Europe, Canada, and the U.S.

stories.title: DOJ expands IRS settlement to permanently block Trump tax audits
stories.description: Acting AG Todd Blanche signed an addendum Tuesday shielding Trump, his family, and businesses from IRS audits of past tax returns.

stories.title: GOP lawmakers skewer Pentagon over canceled Poland deployment
stories.description: House Armed Services Committee members blasted Army leaders Friday over Defense Secretary Hegseth's abrupt cancellation of a 4,000-troop rotation to Poland.

stories.title: Anthropic agrees to $30B round at $900B valuation
stories.description: The deal, expected to close this month, would nearly triple the AI startup's valuation from three months ago and surpass rival OpenAI.

stories.title: Thune breaks with Trump over $1.8B 'anti-weaponization' fund
stories.description: Senate Majority Leader said Tuesday he's "not a big fan" of the DOJ fund created to settle Trump's dropped $10 billion IRS lawsuit.

stories.title: Pentagon not probing reports of US strikes on Iran schools, hospitals
stories.description: Adm. Brad Cooper told senators the military has no way to verify documented destruction of dozens of civilian sites across Iran.

stories.title: UN demands answers after Taliban detains three journalists
stories.description: UNAMA expressed "serious concern" over the arrests of three Afghan journalists on unspecified charges, calling for due process protections.

stories.title: Vance takes White House briefing room podium amid 2028 jockeying
stories.description: The vice president fielded questions on Iran, primary elections, and Trump's agenda while filling in for press secretary Karoline Leavitt.

stories.title: Supreme Court clears way for Texas' 600th execution
stories.description: The court overturned a stay for Edward Busby, whom experts on both sides have deemed intellectually disabled, despite a 2002 ban on such executions.

stories.title: Six Arab nations demand Iran withdraw Hormuz claims in UN letter
stories.description: Bahrain, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Qatar, and Jordan issued six demands including the strait's unconditional reopening and compensation for damages.

stories.title: Treasury's top lawyer quits over Trump's $1.8B settlement fund
stories.description: Brian Morrissey resigned as Treasury general counsel hours after the DOJ created the "Anti-Weaponization Fund" from Trump's IRS lawsuit settlement.

stories.title: Yemen's warring sides agree to largest prisoner swap since civil war began
stories.description: More than 1,600 detainees will be released after 14 weeks of UN-brokered negotiations in Amman, Jordan.

stories.title: Bolivian miners clash with police in La Paz, demand president's resignation
stories.description: Violent clashes erupted Thursday as mining groups tried to storm Plaza Murillo amid a deepening economic crisis and general strike.

stories.title: Trump orders DOJ probe into Maryland mail ballot error
stories.description: The president called the ballots "illegal," but state officials say a vendor printing mistake caused some voters to receive the wrong party's ballot.

stories.title: Supreme Court preserves mail access for abortion pill mifepristone
stories.description: The justices preserved telehealth and mail access to mifepristone while Louisiana's challenge to FDA dispensing rules proceeds in lower courts.

stories.title: Patel took 'VIP snorkel' at Pearl Harbor on FBI trip, emails show
stories.description: Government emails obtained by the AP reveal the FBI omitted a special snorkeling excursion around the sunken USS Arizona from its public statements.

stories.title: U.S. sanctions Cuba's intelligence agency and nine officials
stories.description: The Treasury Department targeted Cuba's spy service and senior government figures, including three cabinet ministers, under Trump's expanded sanctions framework.

stories.title: IPC says nearly 20 million in Sudan face acute hunger
stories.description: The global hunger monitor's new assessment finds over 40% of Sudan's population in severe food insecurity as the civil war enters its fourth year.

stories.title: Senate votes 99-0 to advance pay freeze for senators during shutdowns
stories.description: The resolution would place senators' paychecks in escrow during any future government funding lapse, with final passage expected in coming days.

stories.title: Trump says US, Nigerian forces killed ISIS's global second-in-command
stories.description: Abu-Bilal al-Minuki was killed in a joint operation in Nigeria, according to Trump, who called it a major blow to the militant group.

stories.title: Border Patrol Chief Banks resigns effective immediately
stories.description: The abrupt departure follows allegations by six agents that Banks engaged in sex tourism abroad over the course of a decade.

stories.title: About 30 vessels cross Strait of Hormuz with Iran's approval amid standoff
stories.description: Iran authorized roughly 30 ships to transit the contested waterway since Wednesday, state TV reported, as Chinese, Japanese, and Indian vessels push through dueling blockades.

stories.title: Senate Democrats slam Trump's silence on Taiwan arms sale at Beijing summit
stories.description: Lawmakers called Trump's refusal to raise a $14 billion pre-approved arms package with Xi Jinping "a critical step backwards" for U.S. interests.

stories.title: Streeting resigns as health secretary, launches bid to unseat Starmer
stories.description: The resignation plunges Labour deeper into crisis as Streeting needs 81 MPs to formally trigger a leadership contest.

stories.title: ICC-wanted senator flees Philippine Senate after gunfire
stories.description: Sen. Ronald dela Rosa escaped the building where he had sheltered since Monday, with police investigating whether the shooting was staged to cover his exit.

stories.title: Trump calls Takaichi from Air Force One after China summit
stories.description: The 15-minute call reaffirmed the U.S.-Japan alliance as questions mount over Trump's handling of Taiwan during talks with Xi.

stories.title: Webb telescope spots most primitive galaxy ever found
stories.description: A 13-billion-year-old galaxy with just 1/240th the Sun's oxygen content is the closest match yet to fossil galaxies near the Milky Way.

stories.title: Russia's massive drone attack forces Slovakia to briefly shut Ukraine border
stories.description: At least 800 drones targeted western Ukraine, striking Uzhhorod near the Slovak frontier and prompting Hungary to summon Russia's ambassador.

stories.title: China declines Nvidia H200 chips despite US clearance, Trump says
stories.description: Trump said Beijing "chose not to" buy approved AI chips, preferring to develop domestic alternatives amid a complex web of export conditions.

stories.title: Rutte proposes NATO allies spend 0.25% of GDP on Ukraine aid annually
stories.description: The plan could nearly triple collective support to about $143 billion a year, but France and the U.K. are already pushing back.

stories.title: UAE denies Netanyahu's claimed secret visit during Iran war
stories.description: Netanyahu's office said he met Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed in Al-Ain on March 26, but the UAE called the claim "entirely unfounded."

stories.title: US allies forge closer China ties as Trump wraps Beijing summit
stories.description: From Canada to the UK to Australia, Washington's partners have independently deepened trade relationships with Beijing amid tariff uncertainty.

stories.title: FAA to spend $16.5M on airport vehicle transponders after fatal LaGuardia crash
stories.description: The move will equip about 1,900 vehicles at 264 airports so controllers can track ground traffic on runways and taxiways.

stories.title: Moscow bans media from publishing drone strike aftermath
stories.description: The city's Anti-Terrorism Commission bars residents, media, and emergency services from sharing attack images until they appear in official channels.

stories.title: Trump leaves Beijing without Iran breakthrough after two-day Xi summit
stories.description: The leaders agreed Iran must not obtain nuclear weapons, but China's statement diverged from the White House version, leaving no clear path to end the 77-day war.

top.title: SPLC asks judge to toss DOJ indictment, alleging vindictive prosecution
top.description: Lawyers argue the 11-count case is the "culmination of a top-down, retributive campaign" directed by President Trump against political enemies.

top.title: Cuba calls US oil blockade 'an act of war' at UN Security Council
top.description: Foreign Minister Bruno Rodríguez Parrilla urged the international community to mobilize against what he called an unfolding humanitarian catastrophe.

top.title: UK and Poland to sign defense treaty targeting Russian threats
top.description: The agreement, set for Wednesday in London, will strengthen border security, combat organized crime, and jointly develop advanced weapons including air defense missiles.

top.title: Paxton defeats Cornyn in Texas Senate primary runoff
top.description: Trump-backed Attorney General Ken Paxton ousted four-term Sen. John Cornyn in the costliest Senate primary in U.S. history.

top.title: UN Security Council condemns drone strike on UAE nuclear plant
top.description: The council unanimously called the May 17 attack on Barakah a violation of international law but did not assign blame.

top.title: US plans quarantine facility in Kenya for Ebola-exposed Americans
top.description: The site, pending Kenyan approval, would house Americans at risk of or infected with the Bundibugyo strain spreading in the DRC, according to The Wall Street Journal.

top.title: Two Democratic incumbents face off in Texas runoff forced by GOP redistricting
top.description: Reps. Al Green and Christian Menefee are competing Tuesday for a single Houston seat after Republicans redrew the congressional map.

top.title: Rubio aide Needham promoted to deputy national security adviser
top.description: The move deepens Rubio's hold on national security policy as he continues serving as both secretary of state and acting NSA.

top.title: DOJ sues UCLA over antisemitism in third suit against UC this year
top.description: The lawsuit alleges the university was "deliberately indifferent" to harassment of Jewish and Israeli students during a 2024 pro-Palestinian encampment.

top.title: Iran calls U.S. strikes a ceasefire breach as Qatar talks continue
top.description: Tehran warned it "will not leave any act of aggression without a response" after the U.S. hit missile sites and mine-laying vessels near Bandar Abbas.

top.title: South Carolina Senate kills Trump-backed redistricting plan
top.description: Record early voting turnout helped sway Republicans to reject new congressional maps targeting Rep. Jim Clyburn's district.

top.title: Florida judge allows GOP-drawn congressional map for 2026 midterms
top.description: Voting rights groups vow to appeal to the Florida Supreme Court after a DeSantis-appointed judge declined to block the Republican-favored districts.

top.title: Israel intensifies Lebanon strikes as US-Iran deal nears
top.description: Netanyahu announced expanded attacks on Hezbollah as Israeli airstrikes killed at least 11 in Lebanon's Bekaa Valley amid US-Iran negotiations.

top.title: Trump claims 'perfect' results after physical at Walter Reed
top.description: The 79-year-old president underwent his third medical evaluation in 13 months, but no official report had been released as of Tuesday afternoon.

top.title: Iran's judiciary blocks presidential order to restore internet
top.description: President Pezeshkian ordered international internet access restored after a near-90-day blackout, but the judiciary suspended the body tasked with carrying it out.

top.title: Supreme Court rejects Florida's bid to sue over immigrant truck licenses
top.description: The justices declined to hear the state-versus-state dispute over commercial driver's licenses issued to undocumented immigrants.

top.title: Trump administration proposes NDAs for all federal workers
top.description: OPM published a draft nondisclosure agreement in the Federal Register on Tuesday, targeting leaks of internal government information to the media.

top.title: Pentagon accepts SpaceX price hike for drone connectivity amid Iran war
top.description: The military nearly doubled what it pays per attack drone after SpaceX argued its Starlink network was being used at a discount during active combat.

top.title: BP removes chairman Albert Manifold over conduct concerns
top.description: The board cited "unacceptable" governance lapses and appointed Ian Tyler as interim chair, just eight months into Manifold's tenure.

top.title: Supreme Court backs Trump on immigration judge speech curbs
top.description: The justices reversed a Fourth Circuit ruling on procedural grounds without addressing whether the speech restrictions are constitutional.

top.title: Zelensky says US missile defense talks have stalled
top.description: Ukraine's president declared air defense Kyiv's top priority and said he is accelerating efforts to produce anti-ballistic systems in Europe instead.

top.title: Cuba publishes names of 2,000 pardoned prisoners
top.description: The full list, released Monday in the official gazette, enables rights groups to verify whether any political detainees were included in the April amnesty.

top.title: Massie files for 2028 race days after Trump-backed primary defeat
top.description: The Kentucky congressman declined to rule out a presidential bid, saying he hasn't decided which office to seek.

top.title: Putin signs decree canceling debts for new Ukraine war recruits
top.description: The Kremlin is offering up to 10 million roubles in debt relief to those who enlist for at least one year, along with their spouses.

top.title: Iran says Khamenei suffered only 'superficial' wounds in February airstrikes
top.description: Health Ministry officials described the supreme leader's injuries as minor, contradicting months of reports that he was in a coma or lost a leg.

top.title: Iran would reopen Strait of Hormuz 30 days after deal, Nikkei reports
top.description: Tehran would clear mines from the strategic waterway during the post-agreement window, ending transit fees for all ships, according to the report.

top.title: Lavrov urges Rubio to evacuate US embassy from Kyiv
top.description: Russia warned of "systematic strikes" on Kyiv's decision-making centers and military sites, urging all foreign diplomats to leave immediately.

top.title: Carney calls Alberta separation vote a 'dangerous bluff,' cites Brexit experience
top.description: The prime minister drew on his time leading the Bank of England during Brexit to warn Albertans against treating the October referendum as leverage.

top.title: AI safety filters on Meta, Google models stripped in minutes, FT finds
top.description: Freely available software tools can remove guardrails from major AI models, enabling responses on bioweapons, malware, and exploitation.

top.title: DOJ cites White House shooting to push for ballroom project
top.description: The Justice Department filed a brief Sunday invoking Saturday's fatal shooting near the White House to argue for lifting the injunction on Trump's $400 million ballroom.

top.title: Pope Leo XIV calls to 'disarm' AI in first encyclical
top.description: "Magnifica Humanitas" warns against an unchecked technology race and declares just war theory "outdated" in the age of autonomous weapons.

top.title: Oil and LNG tankers resume Strait of Hormuz transits after three-month halt
top.description: Limited commercial shipping is restarting through the critical chokepoint, though volumes remain far below pre-war levels.

top.title: Inaudible sounds hidden in podcasts can hijack AI voice assistants, researchers find
top.description: Researchers demonstrated that imperceptible audio embedded in everyday media can trick voice AI into unauthorized actions like sending emails or downloading files.

top.title: Trump urges Arab leaders to normalize Israel ties as Iran deal nears
top.description: Arab and Muslim leaders were silent when Trump pressed them to join the Abraham Accords during a call about the pending Iran deal.

top.title: Russia fires Oreshnik missile in massive assault on Kyiv
top.description: The overnight barrage of 90 missiles and 600 drones killed at least four and wounded over 80, Ukrainian officials said.

top.title: Gunman killed after firing on Secret Service near White House
top.description: The suspect, identified as 21-year-old Nasire Best of Maryland, opened fire at a checkpoint Saturday evening. A bystander was critically wounded.

top.title: NATO weighs return to Iraq after wartime evacuation
top.description: The alliance's advisory mission was fully relocated to Italy in March after Iranian strikes on bases in northern Iraq.

top.title: Taiwan says China deployed 100-plus vessels after Trump-Xi summit
top.description: The fleet spans from the Yellow Sea to the South China Sea and western Pacific, marking one of Beijing's largest maritime shows of force in years.

top.title: Iranian hackers posed as recruiters to target US aviation engineers
top.description: Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 uncovered a campaign using fake job ads and compromised video tools to infiltrate aviation and energy networks.

top.title: Zelensky confirms strike on chemical plant 1,700 km inside Russia
top.description: Ukraine's overnight drone campaign hit a Perm Krai chemical plant and a Novorossiysk oil terminal, with Zelensky saying production has been "completely halted."

top.title: DOJ deletes press releases on Jan. 6 charges from its website
top.description: NBC News found the vast majority of news releases about Jan. 6 defendants were removed, as a $1.776 billion compensation fund draws bipartisan scrutiny.

top.title: Zelensky calls Germany's associate EU membership plan 'unfair'
top.description: Ukraine's president told EU leaders the proposal would leave Kyiv "voiceless" and urged full membership instead.

top.title: Democrats demand Rubio explain visa for fugitive Polish minister
top.description: House Democrats called the expedited visa for wanted ex-minister Zbigniew Ziobro "a massive abuse of power" and demanded answers by June 21.

top.title: U.S. has 'almost completely excluded' Israel from Iran talks, officials say
top.description: Israeli defense officials told The New York Times that Washington sidelined Israel from negotiations, deepening the Trump-Netanyahu rift.

top.title: SpaceX launches first Starship V3 but loses booster over Gulf of Mexico
top.description: The 12th test flight debuted SpaceX's most powerful rocket design, but engine failures sent the Super Heavy booster crashing into the Gulf of Mexico.

top.title: Judge tosses Abrego Garcia smuggling charges as vindictive
top.description: The ruling ends the criminal case against the Maryland man whose wrongful deportation to El Salvador's CECOT prison reached the Supreme Court.

top.title: Iran condemns US sanctions on its envoy to Lebanon
top.description: Tehran called the Treasury Department's designation of ambassador Mohammad Reza Sheibani "illegal," accusing Washington of violating international law.

top.title: Trump digs in on 'anti-weaponization' fund as GOP revolt stalls immigration bill
top.description: Senate Republicans blocked a $72 billion immigration bill over the fund, which could pay Jan. 6 defendants. Congress left for recess with no resolution.

top.title: US consumer sentiment hits all-time low in final May reading
top.description: The University of Michigan index fell to 44.8, far below the preliminary 48.2 estimate, as rising gas prices and inflation fears weighed on households.

top.title: All 'Broadview Six' charges dropped after grand jury misconduct
top.description: A federal judge dismissed all remaining cases against the ICE protest defendants with prejudice after a closed-door hearing revealed prosecutorial errors.

top.title: Lutnick gave $5M to House GOP weeks before Epstein testimony
top.description: An FEC filing made public Thursday shows the commerce secretary donated to the Congressional Leadership Fund on April 1, a month before his closed-door interview.

top.title: Russia developing seabed nuclear missiles, German investigation finds
top.description: NATO is monitoring a secret Russian program to hide remote-controlled nuclear missiles on the Arctic Ocean floor, as Moscow wraps up its largest nuclear drills in years.

top.title: 75% of Democratic voters oppose military aid to Israel, poll finds
top.description: A New York Times/Siena poll shows opposition nearly doubled from 45% three years ago, reflecting deepening frustration over Gaza and the US-Israel war on Iran.

top.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as intelligence chief, citing husband's cancer
top.description: The director of national intelligence is stepping down effective June 30 to support her husband's battle with a rare form of bone cancer.

top.title: Rubio says diplomatic solution with Cuba unlikely after Castro indictment
top.description: The secretary of state cited Russian and Chinese intelligence presence in Cuba as a national security threat, leaving military action on the table.

top.title: Sen. Sullivan asks donors for gas money after backing Iran war
top.description: The Alaska Republican's fundraising email drew criticism as fuel prices soar due to the conflict he has repeatedly voted to continue.

top.title: Trump adviser sought Commerce ban on Dominion voting machines
top.description: A Reuters investigation revealed that election-security czar Kurt Olsen asked Commerce to declare Dominion components a national security risk.

top.title: Iran seeks permanent Hormuz toll system in talks with Oman
top.description: Tehran's new strait authority declared a "controlled maritime zone" requiring all vessels to obtain authorization before transiting.

top.title: Lithuania confirms crashed drone was Ukrainian
top.description: Lithuania's defense ministry said the drone that triggered a shelter alert in Vilnius was Ukrainian, as the military continued searching for it Thursday.

top.title: Trump scraps AI executive order after tech CEO pushback
top.description: The president called off the signing hours before a planned ceremony, saying he didn't want to jeopardize the U.S. lead over China.

top.title: DNC releases long-delayed 2024 election autopsy report
top.description: Chair Ken Martin reversed months of refusals, apologizing for "creating an even bigger distraction" by withholding the document.

top.title: Iran restarted drone production during ceasefire, US intelligence finds
top.description: New assessments say Iran could fully restore drone strike capabilities within six months, contradicting Pentagon claims of lasting damage.

top.title: DOJ files emergency motion to restore sanctions on UN rapporteur Albanese
top.description: After removing UN Special Rapporteur Francesca Albanese from the sanctions list under court order, the Trump administration moved Thursday to reinstate them.

top.title: Turkey sends flights to repatriate Gaza flotilla activists detained by Israel
top.description: Charter flights follow international outcry over Ben-Gvir's video taunting bound detainees, with multiple countries summoning Israeli ambassadors.

top.title: Ukrainian drone strikes bring central Russia's oil refining to a standstill
top.description: Virtually all major refineries in central Russia have halted or reduced output, disrupting a quarter of the country's refining capacity, Reuters reported.

top.title: NATO allies bewildered by Trump's troop reversal
top.description: Trump announced 5,000 troops to Poland weeks after ordering the same number withdrawn, stunning allies at a NATO meeting in Sweden.

top.title: UN General Assembly votes 141-8 to back climate ruling over US objections
top.description: The nonbinding resolution endorses the World Court's opinion that failing to protect the planet from climate change violates international law.

top.title: EU calls treatment of Gaza flotilla activists 'completely unacceptable'
top.description: Israel faces a diplomatic crisis after a video showed Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir taunting handcuffed activists detained from the Gaza-bound flotilla.

top.title: Palestinian envoy drops UN vice presidency bid after US threats
top.description: The Trump administration warned it could revoke the Palestinian delegation's US visas if Ambassador Riyad Mansour did not withdraw.

top.title: DOJ unseals murder indictment against Raúl Castro over 1996 plane shootdown
top.description: Federal prosecutors charged the 94-year-old former Cuban president at a Miami ceremony marking the 30th anniversary of the attack that killed four Cuban Americans.

top.title: OpenAI prepares to submit confidential IPO filing within days
top.description: The AI company is reportedly working with banks on a prospectus filing days after a jury unanimously dismissed Elon Musk's lawsuit.

top.title: DHS warns it could halt customs at sanctuary city airports
top.description: Secretary Mullin privately told travel executives that airports in eight major cities could lose international flight processing.

top.title: Barney Frank, gay rights pioneer in Congress, dies at 86
top.description: The former Massachusetts congressman co-authored the Dodd-Frank Act and was the first member of Congress to voluntarily come out as gay.

top.title: Samsung union suspends strike after last-minute deal
top.description: Nearly 48,000 workers were set to walk out Thursday before Labor Minister Kim Young-hoon brokered a tentative agreement on bonus demands.

top.title: House GOP pulls Iran war powers vote as absences risk defeat
top.description: Republican leaders canceled a Thursday vote on limiting Trump's Iran military campaign after party absences threatened to hand Democrats a majority.

top.title: US and Israel planned to install Ahmadinejad after Iran war began
top.description: A New York Times report reveals the regime-change strategy collapsed on its first day when the former Iranian president lost faith after a close-call airstrike.

top.title: Putin hails 'unprecedented' Russia-China ties in Beijing talks with Xi
top.description: The summit came days after Trump's own visit to Beijing, underscoring China's role as a diplomatic bridge between Washington and Moscow.

top.title: Trump calls birthright citizenship 'a disgrace' ahead of Supreme Court ruling
top.description: The president urged justices to "do what's right" while criticizing the court over its earlier decision striking down his tariff regime.

top.title: Booker wins Kentucky Democratic Senate primary
top.description: The progressive former state legislator will face Trump-backed Rep. Andy Barr in November for Mitch McConnell's open seat.

top.title: Chile's Kast fires security minister in first cabinet shake-up
top.description: President José Antonio Kast removed two ministers Tuesday amid sliding approval ratings and criticism over his government's handling of crime.

top.title: Trump administration awards $2B to quantum firms, takes equity stakes
top.description: The Commerce Department is funding nine quantum-computing companies while acquiring ownership positions, aiming to counter China's tech influence.

top.title: Tuberville, Jones win Alabama primaries, setting up rematch
top.description: The former Senate rivals will face off again in November after each won their party's gubernatorial primary on Tuesday.

top.title: Drone strike kills 28 at crowded market in Sudan
top.description: The attack on a market in West Kordofan's Ghabesh town wounded dozens more, a local rights group said, calling it a war crime.

top.title: Iran's Supreme Leader orders enriched uranium to stay in country
top.description: The directive contradicts a key U.S. demand and complicates peace talks that Trump said Wednesday were in their "final stages."

top.title: Shapiro, Garrity win primaries, setting up Pa. governor race
top.description: Democratic Gov. Josh Shapiro and Republican Treasurer Stacy Garrity cleared uncontested primaries Tuesday, officially teeing up a November showdown.

top.title: Survivors say Army ignored medical aid request before fatal Kuwait drone strike
top.description: Soldiers told CBS News a killed service member could have been saved had their unit received the additional medical support it requested.

top.title: Beijing delays Pentagon official's visit over Taiwan arms deal
top.description: China is stalling a planned trip by Elbridge Colby, the Pentagon's No. 3 official, to pressure Trump on a $14 billion Taiwan weapons package, the FT reports.

top.title: Meta's Louisiana AI data center could cost $200B
top.description: Bloomberg reports the Hyperion facility in rural Richland Parish will need 10 new gas plants and up to 7.5 gigawatts of power.

top.title: Pentagon watchdog to investigate US boat strikes in Caribbean
top.description: The Inspector General's probe is the first formal internal review of Operation Southern Spear, which has killed nearly 200 people since September 2025.

top.title: Judge orders White House staff to comply with Presidential Records Act
top.description: A federal judge rejected the DOJ's claim that the Watergate-era records law is unconstitutional, issuing a preliminary injunction taking effect May 26.

top.title: China secretly trained 200 Russian troops now fighting in Ukraine, Reuters reports
top.description: The revelation emerged as Putin arrived in Beijing for a two-day summit with Xi, marking a new threshold in the two nations' military ties.

top.title: Pelosi endorses Connie Chan to succeed her in Congress
top.description: The retiring former House speaker backed the San Francisco supervisor in a tight race ahead of the June 2 primary.

top.title: Jan. 6 officers sue to block DOJ's $1.8B anti-weaponization fund
top.description: Retired Capitol Police officer Harry Dunn and D.C. officer Daniel Hodges filed a federal lawsuit Wednesday arguing the taxpayer-backed program is unconstitutional.

top.title: Mexico, Uruguay aid ship docks in Havana amid Cuba crisis
top.description: The Asian Katra cargo vessel arrived Monday carrying food and hygiene supplies as blackouts exceed 20 hours daily in parts of Havana.

top.title: Supreme Court to decide if employees can sue under Title IX
top.description: The justices agreed Monday to hear a case testing whether college coaches and professors can bring sex discrimination claims under the federal law.

top.title: Xi and Putin condemn Golden Dome at Beijing summit
top.description: The two leaders signed roughly 40 agreements and issued a joint statement calling Trump's missile defense plan a threat to global stability.

top.title: New York Times files second lawsuit over Pentagon escort rule
top.description: The newspaper argues the policy requiring journalist escorts inside the Pentagon is "patently unconstitutional" and retaliatory.

top.title: Jury sides with OpenAI, rejects Musk's lawsuit
top.description: The nine-member federal jury in Oakland found Musk's case was filed too late, removing a major legal threat to OpenAI's for-profit future.

top.title: IRGC warns war will spread beyond Middle East if US resumes strikes
top.description: The threat came as tankers exited the Strait of Hormuz and Trump said the conflict could end "very quickly."

top.title: ICE agent charged in shooting during Minnesota immigration crackdown
top.description: Hennepin County Attorney Mary Moriarty announced charges Monday against the officer who shot a Venezuelan man during Operation Metro Surge.

top.title: DOJ drops criminal charges against Adani, ending all three U.S. cases
top.description: The dismissal, a $275 million Treasury settlement, and an SEC deal resolve all U.S. legal proceedings against the Indian billionaire.

top.title: Pentagon warnings on Iran's air defenses shaped Trump's strike pause
top.description: Trump called off a planned attack on Iran after Pentagon officials warned that Tehran had learned to track U.S. warplane flight patterns, the Times reported.

top.title: Cuba warns of 'bloodbath' after U.S. drone threat report
top.description: President Díaz-Canel said Monday that U.S. military action against Cuba would have "incalculable consequences," denying Havana poses any threat.

top.title: Judge allows gun, notebook as evidence in Mangione trial
top.description: The ruling is a major win for prosecutors ahead of the September state murder trial for the killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.

top.title: Trump-backed challengers oust Massie, Raffensperger in Tuesday primaries
top.description: The president's retribution campaign swept primary night as his endorsed candidates toppled GOP critics across Kentucky and Georgia.

top.title: Pakistan sends 8,000 troops, jets to Saudi Arabia
top.description: Reuters reports Pakistan deployed fighter jets, drones, and a Chinese air defense system under a mutual defense pact while mediating the Iran war.

top.title: NextEra, Dominion agree to $67B merger forming largest U.S. utility
top.description: The all-stock deal would unite 10 million customer accounts across four states amid surging power demand from AI data centers.

top.title: EU reaches deal to cut US import duties ahead of Trump's tariff deadline
top.description: Negotiators from the European Parliament and Council struck a provisional agreement early Wednesday in Strasbourg, clearing the way for a ratification vote in June.

top.title: Saudi Arabia intercepts three drones from Iraqi airspace
top.description: The kingdom vowed to take "necessary operational measures" to defend its sovereignty as drone threats persist despite the April ceasefire.

top.title: Two Navy Growlers collide mid-air at Idaho air show
top.description: All four crew members ejected safely after two EA-18G Growler jets crashed during the Gunfighter Skies Air Show at Mountain Home Air Force Base.

top.title: Trump's Gaza board warns of funding gap in UN report
top.description: The Board of Peace said $17 billion remains pledged but did not disclose how much has been received for the $70 billion reconstruction effort.

top.title: All 46 Council of Europe nations adopt migration declaration
top.description: The non-binding declaration reinterprets the European Convention on Human Rights to ease deportations, including to third-country "return hubs."

top.title: DHS forms unit targeting green card holders for deportation
top.description: A newly created "Tactical Operations Division" has reviewed roughly 2,890 cases and is seeking to deport at least 50 lawful permanent residents.

top.title: Trump drops plan requiring banks to collect citizenship data
top.description: The president signed two executive orders Tuesday, replacing a months-long push for a bank citizenship mandate with softer guidance.

top.title: US suspects Iran hacked fuel tank monitors at gas stations
top.description: Hackers exploited unprotected automatic tank gauges across multiple states, manipulating display readings but not actual fuel levels, CNN reports.

top.title: Kataib Hezbollah commander charged in plot against U.S. synagogue, Jewish centers
top.description: Al-Saadi, arrested in Turkey, faces six terrorism counts for allegedly directing 20 attacks across Europe, Canada, and the U.S.

top.title: DOJ expands IRS settlement to permanently block Trump tax audits
top.description: Acting AG Todd Blanche signed an addendum Tuesday shielding Trump, his family, and businesses from IRS audits of past tax returns.

top.title: GOP lawmakers skewer Pentagon over canceled Poland deployment
top.description: House Armed Services Committee members blasted Army leaders Friday over Defense Secretary Hegseth's abrupt cancellation of a 4,000-troop rotation to Poland.

top.title: Anthropic agrees to $30B round at $900B valuation
top.description: The deal, expected to close this month, would nearly triple the AI startup's valuation from three months ago and surpass rival OpenAI.

top.title: Thune breaks with Trump over $1.8B 'anti-weaponization' fund
top.description: Senate Majority Leader said Tuesday he's "not a big fan" of the DOJ fund created to settle Trump's dropped $10 billion IRS lawsuit.

top.title: Pentagon not probing reports of US strikes on Iran schools, hospitals
top.description: Adm. Brad Cooper told senators the military has no way to verify documented destruction of dozens of civilian sites across Iran.

top.title: UN demands answers after Taliban detains three journalists
top.description: UNAMA expressed "serious concern" over the arrests of three Afghan journalists on unspecified charges, calling for due process protections.

top.title: Vance takes White House briefing room podium amid 2028 jockeying
top.description: The vice president fielded questions on Iran, primary elections, and Trump's agenda while filling in for press secretary Karoline Leavitt.

top.title: Supreme Court clears way for Texas' 600th execution
top.description: The court overturned a stay for Edward Busby, whom experts on both sides have deemed intellectually disabled, despite a 2002 ban on such executions.

top.title: Six Arab nations demand Iran withdraw Hormuz claims in UN letter
top.description: Bahrain, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Qatar, and Jordan issued six demands including the strait's unconditional reopening and compensation for damages.

top.title: Treasury's top lawyer quits over Trump's $1.8B settlement fund
top.description: Brian Morrissey resigned as Treasury general counsel hours after the DOJ created the "Anti-Weaponization Fund" from Trump's IRS lawsuit settlement.

top.title: Yemen's warring sides agree to largest prisoner swap since civil war began
top.description: More than 1,600 detainees will be released after 14 weeks of UN-brokered negotiations in Amman, Jordan.

top.title: Bolivian miners clash with police in La Paz, demand president's resignation
top.description: Violent clashes erupted Thursday as mining groups tried to storm Plaza Murillo amid a deepening economic crisis and general strike.

top.title: Trump orders DOJ probe into Maryland mail ballot error
top.description: The president called the ballots "illegal," but state officials say a vendor printing mistake caused some voters to receive the wrong party's ballot.

top.title: Supreme Court preserves mail access for abortion pill mifepristone
top.description: The justices preserved telehealth and mail access to mifepristone while Louisiana's challenge to FDA dispensing rules proceeds in lower courts.

top.title: Patel took 'VIP snorkel' at Pearl Harbor on FBI trip, emails show
top.description: Government emails obtained by the AP reveal the FBI omitted a special snorkeling excursion around the sunken USS Arizona from its public statements.

top.title: U.S. sanctions Cuba's intelligence agency and nine officials
top.description: The Treasury Department targeted Cuba's spy service and senior government figures, including three cabinet ministers, under Trump's expanded sanctions framework.

top.title: IPC says nearly 20 million in Sudan face acute hunger
top.description: The global hunger monitor's new assessment finds over 40% of Sudan's population in severe food insecurity as the civil war enters its fourth year.

top.title: Senate votes 99-0 to advance pay freeze for senators during shutdowns
top.description: The resolution would place senators' paychecks in escrow during any future government funding lapse, with final passage expected in coming days.

top.title: Trump says US, Nigerian forces killed ISIS's global second-in-command
top.description: Abu-Bilal al-Minuki was killed in a joint operation in Nigeria, according to Trump, who called it a major blow to the militant group.

top.title: Border Patrol Chief Banks resigns effective immediately
top.description: The abrupt departure follows allegations by six agents that Banks engaged in sex tourism abroad over the course of a decade.

top.title: About 30 vessels cross Strait of Hormuz with Iran's approval amid standoff
top.description: Iran authorized roughly 30 ships to transit the contested waterway since Wednesday, state TV reported, as Chinese, Japanese, and Indian vessels push through dueling blockades.

top.title: Senate Democrats slam Trump's silence on Taiwan arms sale at Beijing summit
top.description: Lawmakers called Trump's refusal to raise a $14 billion pre-approved arms package with Xi Jinping "a critical step backwards" for U.S. interests.

top.title: Streeting resigns as health secretary, launches bid to unseat Starmer
top.description: The resignation plunges Labour deeper into crisis as Streeting needs 81 MPs to formally trigger a leadership contest.

top.title: ICC-wanted senator flees Philippine Senate after gunfire
top.description: Sen. Ronald dela Rosa escaped the building where he had sheltered since Monday, with police investigating whether the shooting was staged to cover his exit.

top.title: Trump calls Takaichi from Air Force One after China summit
top.description: The 15-minute call reaffirmed the U.S.-Japan alliance as questions mount over Trump's handling of Taiwan during talks with Xi.

top.title: Webb telescope spots most primitive galaxy ever found
top.description: A 13-billion-year-old galaxy with just 1/240th the Sun's oxygen content is the closest match yet to fossil galaxies near the Milky Way.

top.title: Russia's massive drone attack forces Slovakia to briefly shut Ukraine border
top.description: At least 800 drones targeted western Ukraine, striking Uzhhorod near the Slovak frontier and prompting Hungary to summon Russia's ambassador.

top.title: China declines Nvidia H200 chips despite US clearance, Trump says
top.description: Trump said Beijing "chose not to" buy approved AI chips, preferring to develop domestic alternatives amid a complex web of export conditions.

top.title: Rutte proposes NATO allies spend 0.25% of GDP on Ukraine aid annually
top.description: The plan could nearly triple collective support to about $143 billion a year, but France and the U.K. are already pushing back.

top.title: UAE denies Netanyahu's claimed secret visit during Iran war
top.description: Netanyahu's office said he met Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed in Al-Ain on March 26, but the UAE called the claim "entirely unfounded."

top.title: US allies forge closer China ties as Trump wraps Beijing summit
top.description: From Canada to the UK to Australia, Washington's partners have independently deepened trade relationships with Beijing amid tariff uncertainty.

top.title: FAA to spend $16.5M on airport vehicle transponders after fatal LaGuardia crash
top.description: The move will equip about 1,900 vehicles at 264 airports so controllers can track ground traffic on runways and taxiways.

top.title: Moscow bans media from publishing drone strike aftermath
top.description: The city's Anti-Terrorism Commission bars residents, media, and emergency services from sharing attack images until they appear in official channels.

top.title: Trump leaves Beijing without Iran breakthrough after two-day Xi summit
top.description: The leaders agreed Iran must not obtain nuclear weapons, but China's statement diverged from the White House version, leaving no clear path to end the 77-day war.
